findings.evidence: `rg` shows the independent binary proof at lines 128-148 and the general nonempty supremum theorem at lines 152-203; binary joins are the nonempty supremum of a two-element family.
findings.fix: Prove `comap_iSup_of_surjective` first and derive `comap_sup_of_surjective` from it using a two-element index such as `Bool`/`Fin 2`, or otherwise make the binary lemma a thin wrapper around the general theorem.
findings.issue: `comap_sup_of_surjective` is a binary special case of the later nonempty-family theorem `comap_iSup_of_surjective`, but it carries a separate elementwise proof.

summary: The surjective comap itself sits at the intended level, but the API still proves a special-case join theorem independently despite also proving the corresponding nonempty supremum theorem. Derive the binary join lemma from the general `iSup` result instead of maintaining duplicate proof logic.
